articles.description: Airports generate not only passenger traffic but build economic ecosystems. Jewar is now at the beginning of that curve.

articles.title: Manufacturing PMI slows to 54.2 in June, second-lowest in four years
articles.description: Manufacturing PMI in India slows to 54.2 in June, marking the second-lowest level in four years amid declining orders.

articles.title: Reliance Power shares surge 18% on foraying into AI, allied new-age technologies
articles.description: The rally came after the company, in an exchange filing, said it has undertaken certain enabling steps through its subsidiaries to participate in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence and allied new-age technologies.

articles.title: 'Bullying was ignored, Bible readings were routine': Parent's post on Bengaluru international school is viral
articles.description: The account, shared anonymously on Reddit, was presented as a caution to families considering relocating to India with school-age children. The parent urged others to speak with families already associated with prospective schools before making enrolment decisions, arguing that marketing material and university admission records do not necessarily reflect students' day-to-day experiences.

articles.title: Govt Notifies Revised Wage Rates As VB-G RAM G Act Comes Into Force Today
articles.description: A new interim base wage rate of Rs 300 per day has also been introduced, ensuring no notified wage falls below that level, with 21 states and administrative units brought up to this floor.

articles.title: Royal Enfield sales up 27 pc to 1,14,032 motorcycles in June
articles.description: Royal Enfield revved up sales in June 2026, reporting a significant 27% surge to 1,14,032 motorcycles. This growth is attributed to strong demand for their updated models. Adding to the excitement, the company has begun delivering its groundbreaking electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, in Bengaluru, with plans for a wider rollout. Management expresses optimism for continued sales momentum.

articles.title: From Energy to AI Enabler: Bloom Energy becomes market’s hot pick
articles.description: Bloom Energy shares jumped over 20% after Brookfield Asset Management expanded its financing commitment to $25 billion, boosting optimism over AI-driven data centre power demand and infrastructure growth.

articles.description: MCX share price surged as much as 5.14% to Rs 2,982.6 apiece by 10:22 am.

articles.description: Sharran Srivatsaa rose from janitor to CEO despite being told his accent limited job prospects.

articles.title: Positive Breakout: These 11 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
articles.description: As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily time frame, it is generally considered to be an overall uptrend.

articles.title: Trading Plan: Will Nifty 50, Bank Nifty snap their two-day losing streak on the first day of July series amid range-bound trading?
articles.description: In case of a bounce-back, the Nifty 50 is likely to face resistance in the 24,000-24,100 zone. However, support is placed at 23,800, as a fall below this level could bring the bears to the forefront. Overall, the trading range for the index remains at 23,800-24,200 going ahead.

articles.title: The Burnham blueprint, as told by one of its architects – podcast
articles.description: Andy Burnham has set out his economic plans for Britain. Neal Lawson , from the Burnham-aligned group Mainstream, explains the thinking behind it Helen Pidd has been one of Andy Burnham’s constituents for almost a decade now. She says she has seen Greater Manchester thrive on his watch. Now he wants to do for the rest of the country what he has done for her region. On Monday Burnham stood up in the People’s History Museum in Manchester and delivered his economic manifesto. Barring some 11th hour challenge or unforeseen calamity, he will have moved into No 10 by the end of July. But he says he wants to build another No 10. A Manchester one. What he is promising is radical, but just how credible is “rewiring” Britain? Neal Lawson is the founder of pressure group Compass, and more recently the Burnham-aligned Mainstream. He explains to Helen why Burnham is so keen to devolve power and what he wants to nationalise. But, he says, “I think what Burnham has done is kind of, by a sort of minor miracle, reach base camp. And now there’s the mountain to climb.” Continue reading...

The Dow had its biggest quarterly jump since 2022. articles.title: Quote of the Day by Steve Jobs: ‘It's not a faith in technology...’
articles.description: Steve Jobs (1955-2011) was an American entrepreneur, inventor, and business magnate best known as the co-founder of Apple. Jobs played a key role in revolutionising personal computing, smartphones, digital music, and animation.

articles.title: 'I have everything figured out': Microsoft employee says manager's silence hinted at layoff, calls it 'relief'
articles.description: A Microsoft employee expecting to be laid off shared a viral post saying they feel relieved rather than upset, claiming their manager had been giving them fewer responsibilities and less feedback, which made the layoff seem inevitable.

articles.title: SBI employee gets relief in harassment case as Bombay HC rules shared auto commute falls outside POSH law’s workplace definition
articles.description: The Bombay High Court has overturned disciplinary action against an SBI employee, ruling that a shared auto-rickshaw is not a 'workplace' under the POSH Act. The employee faced harassment charges after an incident during his commute. The court emphasized that for the Act to apply, transportation must be employer-provided. This decision leaves the merits of the harassment allegation open for other legal avenues.

articles.title: India's UPI goes live in Greece with Eurobank, Piyush Goyal attends
articles.description: Union Minister Piyush Goyal witnessed the launch of India's UPI services at Eurobank headquarters in Athens. The partnership enables instant, secure, and low-cost money transfers, marking a significant milestone in India's global digital expansion.

articles.description: The Biden administration's SAVE plan will phase out, requiring borrowers to switch to new options, potentially resulting in higher payments.

articles.title: AM/NS takes over banks' claims linked to Essar Group promoters' personal guarantees
articles.description: "Banks had claims attached to the personal guarantees issued by the Essar promoters which was linked to the Essar Steel resolution. AM/NS' plan had made specific provisions to pay off these liabilities too, but there were some legal issues which are now resolved, allowing banks and AM/NS to complete this deal," said a person familiar with the details.

articles.title: Stocks in news: Kotak Mahindra Bank, Tata Motors, HDFC Bank, IndiGo, Coal India
articles.description: Indian markets experienced a volatile yet narrow trading range, concluding marginally lower as consolidation persists. Analysts await a decisive breakout above 24,200. Key stocks like Tata Motors, HDFC Bank, IndiGo, Coal India, and Kotak Mahindra Bank are in focus due to significant developments including new product launches, executive appointments, and strategic investments. Coal India plans substantial R&D spending to enhance productivity and reduce emissions.

articles.title: Bandhavgarh sees record 2L tourists
articles.description: Jabalpur: Bandhavgarh Tiger Reserve has recorded its highest-ever tourist footfall and revenue, with more than 2.12 lakh visitors generating around Rs.

articles.title: In Graphics: How GST cuts triggered a rally for Indian businesses
articles.description: Government analysis reveals that last September's Goods and Services Tax rate adjustments led to a significant expansion of the tax base. Higher taxable supplies more than compensated for reduced tax rates, boosting average monthly taxable supply by 22.5% in the latter half of FY26. This positive outcome marks a notable impact as the GST nears its ninth anniversary.

articles.title: Economy resilient but risks from monsoon, geopolitical uncertainties remain: Finance Ministry report
articles.description: Finance Ministry: India's economy remains resilient, but the Finance Ministry warned that uneven monsoon, emerging El Niño conditions and geopolitical uncertainties could weigh on growth, despite easing inflation and strong exports.

articles.title: Noida authority auctions land parcels to Godrej Properties, VRC construction
articles.description: Noida Authority has successfully auctioned two prime land parcels in Sector 151 for residential projects, fetching a total of Rs 666 crore from Godrej Properties and VRC Construction. This development, coupled with the upcoming Noida International Airport and robust infrastructure, is fueling significant growth in the region. Experts predict substantial increases in property values over the next two years, underscoring Noida's burgeoning real estate market.

articles.description: The proposed joint venture has already secured clearance from an inter-ministerial panel, with formal government approval expected soon to operationalise the partnership

articles.description: Our technical analysts identify select stocks every trading day that may gain momentum even in volatile markets. Here are the technical calls for today.

articles.title: Bank credit to industry grows at robust 17.5% in May: RBI
articles.description: Indian banks witnessed a significant surge in credit to industries, with a robust 17.5% annual growth in May. This expansion was driven by accelerated lending to large corporations and sustained healthy growth in the Micro and Small Enterprises (MSE) sector. Advances to agriculture and allied activities also saw a strong uptick. Personal loans and the services sector also recorded impressive growth rates, indicating a vibrant economic landscape.

articles.title: IRDAI chairman urges insurers to build affordable products for Rs 5-25 lakh cover to boost penetration
articles.description: IRDAI Chairman Ajay Seth called for insurance companies to prioritise covering a broader range of unique lives rather than merely chasing premium targets, especially among middle-income families. He stressed the need for simple and affordable insurance products, encouraging insurers to ask if consumers can invest Rs 100-500 monthly for added security.

articles.title: IndiGo Gets New Group HR Head Months After Rostering Row; KS Bakshi Takes Charge In July
articles.description: With over three decades of experience Bakshi is set to take charge from July 20, 2026 and willwork closely with IndiGo's leadership.

articles.title: 'Inflation and unholy mess:' Why Nithin Kamath thinks RBI may not stay soft on interest rates
articles.description: Nithin Kamath warned that a weak monsoon caused by El Niño and rising global oil prices linked to the Iran conflict could sharply increase inflation in India. He said higher food and fuel costs may force the RBI to raise interest rates, hurting growth and market sentiment.

articles.title: India’s ‘401K moment’? HDFC MF CEO Navneet Munot says savers are turning into investors
articles.description: HDFC Mutual Fund CEO Navneet Munot said India’s shift from savings to disciplined investing is becoming a social change, but deeper capital flows are needed.
